subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from provided rulebook text)
- **Base Victory pile size changes** — Estate, Duchy, Province are 8 each in 2-player games and 12 each in 3-4 player games.
- **Curse pile size changes** — 10 Curses (2 players), 20 Curses (3 players), 30 Curses (4 players).
- **Kingdom Victory pile size changes** — Kingdom Victory piles (e.g., Gardens) match base Victory pile size: 8 in 2-player, 12 in 3-4 player.

#### Full Box Inventory
- **Total cards** — 500.
- **Treasure cards** — 130 total (60 Copper, 40 Silver, 30 Gold).
- **Victory cards** — 48 total (24 Estate, 12 Duchy, 12 Province).
- **Curse cards** — 30.
- **Kingdom cards** — 252.
- **Randomizer cards** — 32.
- **Trash card** — 1.
- **Blank cards** — 7.

#### Kingdom Card Structure
- **Kingdom types shown** — 25 different Kingdom card piles.
- **Standard pile size** — 10 cards per selected Kingdom pile.
- **Exception** — Kingdom Victory piles use player-count-dependent Victory sizing.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Deck (every player)
- **Starting cards** — 7 Copper + 3 Estate (10 cards total).
- **Shuffle** — Shuffle these 10 cards to form your deck.
- **Opening hand** — Draw 5 cards.

#### Personal Play Area
- **Deck** — Always face down.
- **Discard pile** — Always face up.
- **In-play area** — Cards played this turn stay here until Clean-up.

#### First Turns Reminder
- **No starting Actions** — You begin without Action cards, so early turns are usually Treasure buys.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Build core Supply** — Place Treasure, Victory, Curse, and Trash area.
2. **Choose Kingdom set** — Select 10 Kingdom piles and place them in Supply.
3. **Determine starting player** — Randomly (or by prior-game rule in multi-game sessions).
4. **Begin play** — Turn order is clockwise.

#### Player-Count Supply Values
--- 2 Players ---
- **Estate pile in Supply** — 8.
- **Duchy pile in Supply** — 8.
- **Province pile in Supply** — 8.
- **Curse pile in Supply** — 10.
- **Kingdom Victory piles (if used)** — 8 cards each.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Estate pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Duchy pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Province pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Curse pile in Supply** — 20.
- **Kingdom Victory piles (if used)** — 12 cards each.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Estate pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Duchy pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Province pile in Supply** — 12.
- **Curse pile in Supply** — 30.
- **Kingdom Victory piles (if used)** — 12 cards each.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Order (always in sequence)
1. **Action Phase**
   - **Base allowance** — Play up to 1 Action card.
   - **+Action effects** — Increase how many Actions you can play this phase.
   - **Resolution rule** — Fully resolve current Action before playing another.
2. **Buy Phase**
   - **Play Treasures** — Play any number from hand.
   - **Count coins** — Add Treasure values plus +coin effects from Actions.
   - **Base buys** — Buy up to 1 card unless +Buys increased this.
   - **Gain destination** — Bought card goes to discard pile unless card text says otherwise.
3. **Clean-up Phase**
   - **Discard in-play cards** — All played Actions and Treasures go to discard.
   - **Discard hand** — All unplayed hand cards go to discard.
   - **Draw** — Draw 5 new cards.
   - **Deck refill** — If deck is short, draw what you can, shuffle discard into new deck, then finish drawing.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action Phase Rules
- **Action play is optional** — You may play zero Actions.
- **Do as much as possible** — If part of an Action cannot happen, resolve every possible part.
- **Card duration in play** — Played Actions remain in play area until Clean-up unless text says otherwise.
- **Reaction exception** — Reaction cards can trigger outside your Action phase when their trigger occurs.

#### Rulebook Term Glossary
- **+X Cards** — Draw X cards immediately (reshuffle discard if needed).
- **+X Actions** — Increase remaining Actions this Action phase.
- **+X coins** — Add spendable coins for this turn's Buy phase.
- **+1 Buy** — Add one additional purchase this Buy phase.
- **Discard** — Move specified card(s) to discard pile (usually from hand).
- **Trash** — Move card(s) to Trash pile, not discard.
- **Gain** — Take card (usually from Supply) and place into discard unless text says otherwise.
- **Reveal** — Show card(s), then return to origin unless text says otherwise.
- **Set Aside** — Place card(s) aside until the effect tells where they go.

#### Buy Phase Rules
- **What can be bought** — Any card in Supply (Treasure, Victory, Kingdom, Curse).
- **What cannot be bought** — Cards in Trash pile.
- **Payment source** — Treasure card values plus +coin effects from Actions this turn.
- **Multiple buys** — If you have +Buys, split coins across legal purchases however you choose.
- **Unused resources allowed** — You may leave buys and coins unused.

#### Card-Flow Clarifications
- **Bought/gained cards** — Usually do not affect the current turn immediately.
- **Treasure persistence** — Treasures are discarded each turn but cycle back when discard is shuffled.
- **Public information** — Top card of each discard pile is visible.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Core Engine Model
- **Shared market** — All players buy from the same Supply piles.
- **Private deck growth** — Your personal deck determines your future options and economy.

#### Buy vs Gain (critical distinction)
- **Buy** — Happens in Buy phase and uses a Buy.
- **Gain** — Usually comes from card effects and does not consume a Buy unless specified.

#### Phase Gates
- **Normal timing** — Actions in Action phase, purchases in Buy phase.
- **Expansion effects** — +Actions, +Buys, +coins, and draw effects alter what becomes possible later that turn.

#### Draw/Shuffle Timing
- **Partial draw first** — Draw remaining deck cards first.
- **Then reshuffle** — Shuffle discard to new deck when needed.
- **Then finish draw** — Complete remaining card draw amount.

#### Setup Variability
- **Kingdom variability** — Each game uses exactly 10 selected Kingdom piles.
- **First-game recommendation** — Cellar, Market, Militia, Mine, Moat, Remodel, Smithy, Village, Woodcutter, Workshop.
- **Selection methods** — Randomizer draw or player-selected sets.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End and Scoring Overview
- **End condition** — Use the base Dominion Supply-depletion end condition from the rules set in use.
- **Scoring scope** — Count points in all owned cards across deck, hand, discard, and in-play area.

#### VP Sources
- **Estate** — 1 VP each.
- **Duchy** — 3 VP each.
- **Province** — 6 VP each.
- **Curse** — -1 VP each.
- **Kingdom Victory cards** — Score as printed when included in your selected 10-pile setup.

#### Winner and Tie Handling
- **Primary winner** — Highest total VP.
- **Tie handling** — Apply official Dominion tie procedure for the rules set in use (commonly fewer turns among tied players).
- **Final tie** — If still tied after official procedure, tied players share victory.

#### Fast Scoring Checklist
1. **Collect all zones** — Deck + hand + discard + in-play.
2. **Add positive VP** — Estates, Duchies, Provinces, and Kingdom VP cards.
3. **Subtract Curses** — Apply -1 per Curse.
4. **Compare totals** — Highest wins, then apply tie procedure.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First-Cycle Priorities
- **Strengthen deck first** — Buy cards that improve future hand quality before buying early VP.
- **Plan Action density** — Add +Actions if you are buying multiple terminal Actions.
- **Stabilize economy** — Ensure reliable coin production in early shuffles.
- **Pair your opening buys** — Treat turns 1-2 purchases as a combined plan for shuffle 1, not isolated choices.

#### Table-Dependent Priorities
- **Check Kingdom set early** — Identify best draw, economy, and gain options in this exact setup.
- **Interaction response** — If attacks/reactions are present, choose early whether to defend, race, or outscale.
- **Tempo awareness** — Prioritize cards that both improve deck quality and keep buy power consistent.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Common Errors and Why They Lose Games
- **Greening too early** — Early Victory cards reduce hand quality before your engine is online.
- **Too many terminal Actions** — Without +Actions support, extra terminal cards sit dead in hand.
- **Ignoring draw/action alignment** — Coin output fails when draw and Action capacity are mismatched.
- **Assuming bought cards are immediate** — Most buys go to discard and do not help the current turn.
- **Missing pile pressure** — Building too long can lose to sudden game end.
- **Skipping trashing tools** — Keeping weak starters bloats deck and lowers consistency.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Engine Growth vs VP Pivot
- **Grow engine** — Buy more consistency/payload cards for stronger future turns.
- **Pivot to points** — Start buying VP when your deck can convert reliably before end triggers.

#### Decision 2: Reliable Package vs High-Ceiling Package
- **Reliable line** — Steady money/draw that performs every shuffle.
- **Explosive line** — Combo cards with higher peak but higher miss risk.

#### Decision 3: Endgame Control
- **Force end** — Push pile pressure/province race when ahead on points now.
- **Delay end** — Extend game when your engine converts better than opponents over extra turns.

metadata.notes: Built from provided Dominion rulebook excerpt with explicit component counts, phase structure, term definitions, and setup rules.
